title: Volume directories in uidmapped/gidmapped containers don't use mapped UID/GID on host
body:
### Issue Description

When running an image that specify volumes with a UID/GID mapping, those directories do not have the right permissons within the container, and the temporary volume created on the host has the original UID/GID from the image, rather than the mapped UID/GID. For example, if a directory `/config` in an image has UID 1000 and GID 1000 and is marked as a volume, and that image is run with `--userns=auto` (with a properly configured mapping for `containers`, then within that container, it'll be owned by 65534/65534, and on the host, it'll be owned by 1000/1000.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Configure a proper mapping for `containers` in `/etc/subuid` and `/etc/subgid`.
2. Run `sudo podman run --userns=auto --entrypoint= --rm docker.io/library/influxdb:latest stat /etc/influxdb2`


### Describe the results you received

```
$ sudo podman run --userns=auto --entrypoint= --rm docker.io/library/influxdb:latest stat /etc/influxdb2
  File: /etc/influxdb2
  Size: 0               Blocks: 0          IO Block: 4096   directory
Device: 0,77    Inode: 9859044     Links: 1
Access: (0755/drwxr-xr-x)  Uid: (65534/  nobody)   Gid: (65534/ nogroup)
Access: 1970-01-01 00:00:00.000000000 +0000
Modify: 2024-10-22 19:55:31.000000000 +0000
Change: 2024-11-07 06:54:41.754888445 +0000
 Birth: 2024-11-07 06:54:41.658886470 +0000
```

```
$ sudo stat /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes/80058cacce51e81f8a6aa2ed957b8a4e5b4956819388f832325c0e6759e30a66/_data
  File: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes/80058cacce51e81f8a6aa2ed957b8a4e5b4956819388f832325c0e6759e30a66/_data
  Size: 0               Blocks: 0          IO Block: 4096   directory
Device: 0,77    Inode: 9859023     Links: 1
Access: (0755/drwxr-xr-x)  Uid: ( 1000/saiarcot895)   Gid: ( 1000/saiarcot895)
Access: 1969-12-31 16:00:00.000000000 -0800
Modify: 2024-10-22 12:55:31.000000000 -0700
Change: 2024-11-06 22:51:58.631527122 -0800
 Birth: 2024-11-06 22:51:58.531525061 -0800
```

### Describe the results you expected

The `/etc/influxdb2` directory should be owned by 1000/1000 within the container, and something like 1049576/1049576 (depending on range) on the host.

comments.body: Setting `image_volume_mode = "ignore"` as a workaround in the `[engine]` section in `/etc/containers/containers.conf` works.
This seems to work fine with the latest version
Can confirm that building and installing version 5.2.5 fixes this.
